t_ch: 几个箱子并不整齐地排列在地上。凭借翻别人快递箱的深厚经验，你一眼就看出，这是几个空箱子。
t_en: A few boxes lay in a messy formation on the ground before you. With your great experience of poking through other people's courier packages, you can tell immediately that they are empty.

t_ch: 为什么要把几个空箱子摆在路边呢？你试着拎起其中一个箱子，却发现他们竟是链接在一起的。
t_en: Why would someone leave empty boxes on the road? You try picking one of them up, and are surprised to find that they are linked together.

t_ch: 换言之，这并不是「几个空箱子」而是「一个奇形怪状的空箱子」。
t_en: In other words, they are not just "a few empty boxes." They are "a strangely-shaped empty box."

t_ch: 这时，你才看到箱子下面写着一行龙飞凤舞的毛笔字——「罗浮当代艺术双年展参展作品：『快递之龙』」。
t_en: At this moment, you notice a line of elegant calligraphy written underneath the boxes: "Biennial Exhibition of Contemporary Luofu Art: Delivery Dragon."

t_ch: 原来这是一件艺术品…好险，你差点就闯大祸了。还是赶紧走吧。
t_en: So, this is an art installation... That was close. You could have made a huge mess. Maybe you should get out of here.

t_ch: 我怎么没看出这是条龙？
t_en: How come I can't tell this is meant to be a dragon?

t_ch: 废话，能让你看出来，那就不叫艺术了。
t_en: Duh. If you could tell, then it would not be art.

t_ch: 原来这就是当代艺术啊…
t_en: So, THIS is what people call contemporary art...

t_ch: 怎么样？有没有感受到艺术对你灵魂的洗涤？
t_en: Well? Do you feel the power of art cleansing your soul?

t_ch: 这什么破玩意儿…
t_en: What garbage is this...

t_ch: 要是能被所有人理解，那就不叫艺术了。
t_en: It would not be art if everyone could understand it.

t_ch: 太美了，「快递之龙」。
t_en: It's beautiful! Delivery Dragon!

t_ch: 是啊，只要凝望着它，就能感受到它那超凡卓绝的艺术表达。
t_en: That's right. Just looking at it would make one feel its extraordinary artistic expression.

t_ch: 「快递箱」正寓意着我们的现代生活，而「龙」则寓意着我们的传统价值。
t_en: The "delivery boxes" are an expression of modern life, whereas the "dragon" is a representation of our traditional values.

t_ch: 也许「快递之龙」并未给出一个答案，但它却提出了一个尖锐的问题——当现代生活与传统价值碰撞时，诞生的究竟是什么呢？
t_en: Perhaps Delivery Dragon cannot give us any answers, but it poses a powerful question: What could be created when modern life collides with traditional values?

t_ch: 总之快离远些吧，这些当代艺术都是坐地起价的，真要不小心搞坏了，你把帕姆卖了都赔不起。
t_en: Either way, best to move away from here. These modern art pieces go for ridiculous prices. If you accidentally break it, even selling Pom-Pom won't cover it!

t_ch: 就在这千钧一发之际…
t_en: Just in the nick of time...

t_ch: 驭空屏息凝神…
t_en: Yukong held her breath...

t_ch: 几个云骑落了地…
t_en: A few Cloud Knights landed...

t_ch: 信仰帝弓司命祂老人家的…
t_en: We of the Xianzhou Alliance are not the only ones who trust in the Reignbow Arbiter...

t_ch: 仙人本为岁阳敌…
t_en: For millennia, the heliobi and Xianzhou fought endlessly as sworn enemy and foe...

t_ch: 说到咱这儿星槎海，就不能不提司辰宫，提到咱这儿司辰宫啊，就不能不提驭空大人。
t_en: You can't talk about Starskiff Haven without mentioning the Palace of Astrum, and you can't talk about the Palace of Astrum without mentioning Madam Yukong.

t_ch: 我以前见过驭空大人手持弯弓「云鸢铣珧」百步穿杨，那场面真是……
t_en: I once saw Madam Yukong with her Cloudkite Resplendent Longbow, shooting with tremendous accuracy from a hundred feet away. Truly a sight to —

t_ch: 你等等，「百步穿杨」？你说我站在离驭空大人一百零一步的地方，可以用手接住飞箭吗？
t_en: Hang on, "a hundred feet"? Are you saying if I stood <unbreak>101</unbreak> feet away, I could just pluck her arrows out of the air?

t_ch: 「百步穿杨」就是一个文学概念，通常来说驭空大人的箭就算飞行<unbreak>1500</unbreak>米也具有十足的威力。
t_en: It's a figure of speech. Generally speaking, Madam Yukong's arrows are a threat from even <unbreak>5000</unbreak> feet.

t_ch: 那我在<unbreak>1501</unbreak>米能用手接住飞箭吗？
t_en: So you're saying if I stood <unbreak>5001</unbreak> feet away, I could catch her arrows.

t_ch: 你为什么非要接那一箭？
t_en: Why do you *want* to catch her arrows?

t_ch: 我是阿往，这位是阿来，今天给大家来一段相声。
t_en: I'm Forth, and this is Back'n, and today we're ready to tickle your funny bone with our crosstalk comedy.

t_ch: 列位观众可能不认识，这位阿往，可是罗浮鼎鼎大名的相声表演艺术家啊。我这次来到咱们星槎海，就是专程为了听您的相声啊！我可算是您的忠实观众。
t_en: You guys may not recognize Forth, but he's a famous crosstalk comedy performer on the Luofu. I came all the way to Starskiff Haven just to watch your performance! I'm a big fan.

t_ch: 哎哟，那可真是谢谢您捧场。那这样吧…您能不能请我吃顿饭？我也不挑嘴，按神策府宴请外宾那个标准就行。
t_en: Well, well, well, look at you being all supportive. My number one fan, huh? How about you treat me to dinner, then? Don't worry, I'm easy to please, but I'm not touching anything the Seat of Divine Foresight wouldn't touch.

t_ch: 不是…凭什么啊？您这怎么一上就骗吃骗喝的？
t_en: But of course, I... Wait, you're thinking about food already? We just started the show — shouldn't we at least finish the first act?

t_ch: 嗐，古人不都说了吗，「观众是演员的衣食父母」啊！衣食父母请我吃一顿饭，合情合理。
t_en: Well, "the audience is the hand that feeds" as they say — and I'm feeling pretty hungry right about now. It's only fitting that "the hand that feeds" treats me to dinner, don't you think?

t_ch: 那你凑过来点儿。
t_en: Hmm, well you'll need to choose, first.

t_ch: 怎么？
t_en: Choose what?

t_ch: 让爸爸抱抱你。
t_en: Left hand or right hand.

t_ch: 你一边待着去吧！
t_en: I'm not hungry anymore.

t_ch: 我是阿往，这位是阿来....
t_en: I'm Forth, and this is Back'n...

t_ch: 我是阿往，这位是阿来....
t_en: I'm Forth, and this is Back'n...

t_ch: 桌上的投影正在卖力地说着评书。它举手投足间的做派和真正的西衍先生一模一样。
t_en: A projection on the table is telling a story animatedly. Its performance style is exactly the same as the real Mr. Xiyan.

t_ch: 桌上的两个投影正在卖力地说着相声。它们举手投足间的做派和真正的阿往与阿来一模一样。
t_en: Two projections on the table are performing a crosstalk comedy animatedly. Their performance style is exactly the same as the real Back'n and Forth.

t_ch: 查看评书话本
t_en: Check storytelling script

t_ch: 查看相声台本
t_en: Check crosstalk script

t_ch: 我小时候常来绥园……
t_en: I was here a lot when I was young...

t_ch: 那时候绥园人还很多。
t_en: Back then, Fyxestroll Garden was the place to go.

t_ch: 你小时候？
t_en: When you were young?

t_ch: 那都多久以前了？
t_en: How long ago was that?

t_ch: 少说也要有<unbreak>700</unbreak>年前了？
t_en: At least <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago?

t_ch: 你以后少说「我小时候」。
t_en: Please stop saying "when I was young."

t_ch: 这世上根本没有幽灵鬼怪…
t_en: There's no such thing as ghosts...

t_ch: 身为十王司不能迷信！
t_en: As a member of the Ten-Lords Commission, one must not be superstitious!

t_ch: 怎么会没有？
t_en: Why not?

t_ch: 你不也经常说「见鬼」吗？
t_en: Weren't you also the one who always says "good heavens!"?

t_ch: 再抬杠就滚蛋！
t_en: If you're only here to play devil's advocate, you can get lost.

t_ch: 你能不能陪我聊聊天？
t_en: Can you stay a while and chat with me?

t_ch: 暂无聊天功能。
t_en: Chat function currently unavailable.

t_ch: 那你随便说点啥也行。
t_en: Well, you can just say anything.

t_ch: 「十恶等罪，狱成之后」…
t_en: "After serving the full term, those who committed any of the Ten Unpardonable Sins..."

t_ch: 「虽会大赦，犹合除名」。
t_en: "...may still face expulsion even if they received general amnesty."

t_ch: 头痛，头痛！莫念，莫念！
t_en: Head hurts... Please stop!

t_ch: 汝今番可听我教诲了？
t_en: Prithee, wouldst thou now hearken to my instruction?

t_ch: …你还是别念了。
t_en: ...Really, please stop.

t_ch: 为了捕获在逃岁阳…
t_en: In order to catch heliobi on the run...

t_ch: 十王司研发了些道具。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission has devised a few... gadgets.

t_ch: 说说都有些什么东西？
t_en: And what, pray tell, might those be?

t_ch: 测量岁阳强度的指示器。
t_en: An indicator device that measures the power level of heliobi.

t_ch: 束缚岁阳的质子背包。
t_en: Proton backpacks that restrict and bind heliobi.

t_ch: 好像在哪听说过的样子…
t_en: Sounds familiar...

t_ch: 发射皮搋子的吸尘器…
t_en: A vacuum cleaner that launches plungers...

t_ch: 这也太搞怪了吧？
t_en: That's just... comical, right?

t_ch: 做人好还是做岁阳好？
t_en: Is it better to be human or to be heliobus?

t_ch: 当然是做人好。
t_en: Human, of course.

t_ch: 那我不做岁阳了！
t_en: So, then I don't wanna be a heliobus!

t_ch: 那你只要找个人寄生就好。
t_en: You just have to leech off of someone, I guess.

t_ch: 对哦，确实。
t_en: That is not untrue.

t_ch: 许多人类坚信世上有鬼。
t_en: There are many who believe in ghosts.

t_ch: 这世上怎么没有鬼？
t_en: Why wouldn't there be ghosts?

t_ch: 咱自己不就是鬼吗？
t_en: Aren't we ghosts ourselves?

t_ch: 咱是岁阳！不是鬼！
t_en: We are heliobi, not ghosts!

t_ch: 你怎么这么迷信呢？
t_en: Why are you so superstitious?

t_ch: 今天遇到一个诗人…
t_en: I met a poet today...

t_ch: 那家伙情绪酸溜溜的。
t_en: That person's mood is so acidic.

t_ch: 我也遇到一个家伙…
t_en: I also met someone...

t_ch: 看上去白白嫩嫩的…
t_en: They look pink and plump...

t_ch: 里外不是一个口味。
t_en: But tasted so gamey.

t_ch: 那咱俩这配套了啊！
t_en: We've got a dinner set!

t_ch: 我有醋，你有饺子。
t_en: I have vinegar, you have potstickers.

t_ch: 帝垣琼玉是个好东西啊！
t_en: Celestial Jade is great!

t_ch: 你附身后光打牌去啦？
t_en: Do you only play tile games after possessing someone?

t_ch: 不是，你不懂。
t_en: No, you don't get it.

t_ch: 人一输牌就血压飙升…
t_en: When someone's on a losing streak, their blood pressure tends to rise...

t_ch: 那时候的口感特别好。
t_en: They taste so good during those times.

t_ch: 你尝过恋爱中的情绪吗？
t_en: Have you ever tasted someone in love?

t_ch: 不太喜欢，有点齁得慌
t_en: Don't like those, they're so sickeningly sweet.

t_ch: 那你可以尝尝失恋。
t_en: Well, you can try the dumped ones.

t_ch: 失恋的味道特别上头。
t_en: The taste of breakups is especially heady.

t_ch: 有句话憋在心里很久了…
t_en: There's something I have to tell you...

t_ch: 我想和你…合二为一。
t_en: I would like us to... two become one.

t_ch: 咱这叫「岁阳融聚」…
t_en: You mean heliobic homogenization...

t_ch: 不叫「合二为一」。
t_en: We don't say "two become one."

t_ch: 你太恶心了，我们俩合不来。
t_en: You're disgusting, we're not a good match.

t_ch: 有个人类妄想与我合作！
t_en: There's a human who fantasizes about cooperating with me!

t_ch: 敢和岁阳谈合作，有胆识。
t_en: Cooperating with a heliobus, they've got some guts.

t_ch: 他要和我合作开烧烤摊。
t_en: He wanted to open a grilled food stall with me.

t_ch: …让我做新能源炭火。
t_en: ...and asked if I could be his renewable flame source.

t_ch: 你是被骗了啊，兄弟。
t_en: ...you got cheated, bud.

t_ch: 看你状态不太好，怎么了？
t_en: You don't look too good, is something the matter?

t_ch: 宿主整天想着成为棉花糖…
t_en: My host spends their days dreaming about becoming a marshmallow...

t_ch: 我也被迫成天想着棉花糖…
t_en: So I'm forced to think about becoming a marshmallow, too...

t_ch: 你别被他的情绪传染了。
t_en: Don't let his emotions infect you.

t_ch: 你说的太晚了…
t_en: Too late...

t_ch: 现在我只想成为棉花糖幽灵。
t_en: Now I just want to be a marshmallow spirit.

t_ch: 我从洪炉里逃出来之后…
t_en: After I escaped from the Furnace...

t_ch: 附身在幻戏制作人身上…
t_en: I possessed an immersia producer...

t_ch: 他正在做《大矿车轨道》。
t_en: He was working on the The Great Minecart Track.

t_ch: 我看到了很多泄露消息！
t_en: I noticed a lot of leaks!

t_ch: 是我看到之后亲自放出去的。
t_en: I was the one who released it after watching him work on it.

t_ch: 那制作人一直骂「见鬼了」。
t_en: The producer just kept cursing.

t_ch: 原来你就是内鬼啊？
t_en: So you're the mole, huh?

t_ch: 最近有帮人自称是我的信徒…
t_en: Lately, there's a gang of people claiming to be my followers...

t_ch: 仙舟人？莫不是诓你的。
t_en: Xianzhou people? They're probably trying to fool you.

t_ch: 不不不，他们要我现身。
t_en: No, no. They just want me to appear before them.

t_ch: 那你露面呗，给他们个惊喜。
t_en: So do it, give them a surprise.

t_ch: 呸，他们要我帮他们做题。
t_en: Pfft, they just want me to help them with their homework.

t_ch: 气得我给他们一顿乱填。
t_en: I was so upset, I gave them all random answers.

t_ch: 我听闻他们的当铺什么都收。
t_en: I hear that pawnshop takes everything.

t_ch: 真的？能典当爱情吗？
t_en: Really? Do they take romance?

t_ch: 你就不能干点正事？
t_en: Can't you just do something serious for once?

t_ch: 我帮人家上学，这不算？
t_en: I'm helping people with school, aren't I?

t_ch: 你就知道在学堂里瞎混…
t_en: You keep faffing around at the lyceum...

t_ch: 我偶尔也会教书育人！
t_en: Sometimes I taught lessons!

t_ch: 我打算换个人附体了。
t_en: I've decided to switch hosts.

t_ch: 为啥，不是挺安逸的吗？
t_en: Why, isn't this one quite peaceful?

t_ch: 哎，那人身上一股味……
t_en: Ugh, the B.O. is strong in that one...

t_ch: 带我们回家吧…
t_en: Bring us home...

t_ch: 或者我们跟你回家…
t_en: Or let us follow you home...

t_ch: 嘿嘿……
t_en: Heh heh...

t_ch: 你相信因果报应吗？
t_en: Do you believe in karma?

t_ch: 别跟着我…
t_en: Stop following me...

t_ch: 让你别跟着我…
t_en: I'm telling you, stop following me...

t_ch: 千万别跟着我…
t_en: Don't follow me around, whatever you do...

t_ch: 我到家了。
t_en: I'm home.

t_ch: 这么多年过去了，仙舟人真是变化太大了。说出来你可能不信，最近是甚至有帮人自称是我的「信徒」，一直缠着要我以真身露面。
t_en: Years have gone by, and the Xianzhou people are so much different than before. This may sound unbelievable, but recently, there's a group of people who fancy themselves my "believers." They have been pestering me to reveal my true form.

t_ch: 仙舟人？得了吧，他们向来很狡猾，再说了哪有人会自称是「信徒」的，莫不是十王司那帮走狗设的局想诓你，你可千万别上当。就算上当了也别把我的位置供出来！
t_en: Xianzhou people? Forget it. They have always been cunning. Then again, why would anyone call themselves "believers"? Could it be a trap set up by the lapdogs of the Ten-Lords Commission? You better be careful. And don't you dare reveal my location!

t_ch: 不不不，他们是脑子里缺根弦。一帮不务正业的年轻人，不知道在哪淘了本书，上面记载了个「见鬼十法」。什么半夜捉迷藏照镜子剁肉馅的，天天就在我耳边吵，一直求我现身。
t_en: No way. They are a bunch of mindless, unemployed youngsters. I don't know where they found a book that recorded the "Ten Ways of Seeing Ghosts." There was something about playing catch in the middle of the night and chopping meat in front of a mirror. Anyway, they kept bothering me and begging me to show up.

t_ch: 听起来是蛮奇怪的，但人坚持了这么久，胜在心诚。既然想见你，你就露个面给呗，给他们个惊喜。
t_en: That's peculiar, but their determination is admirable. I guess it's the heart that counts. Since they wish to meet you, why don't you show your face and give them a nice surprise?

t_ch: 我现身了啊，他们看不见。气死我了，我鼓起劲吹了口气，他们听见了，然后就开始求我帮他们做题。
t_en: I did show up, they just couldn't see me. I got frustrated, so I blew a breath of air at them. They heard me and started asking me to help them with their homework.

t_ch: 「做题」？
t_en: Homework?

t_ch: 对啊，围坐一圈，在桌子上铺了一大张布，上面密密麻麻的全是字。然后要我给他们一一解答，什么「这个数的答案是多少」。
t_en: Yes. The group sat around me. On the table was a large cloth covered with words. They asked me for... the answers, asking questions such as "What's the answer to this problem?"

t_ch: 我又不识字，气得我给他们一顿乱填，呸，识字了不起啊。
t_en: I can't read, so I decided to mess around with them. Meh! So what if they can read?

t_ch: 哎，我这次在罗浮上逛了一圈，真是不堪入目啊。
t_en: Ah, I went to tour around the Luofu this time, and it was really disappointing.

t_ch: 还好吧，这里挺热闹的，来来往往的行商也多，我感觉挺好。
t_en: I thought it was fine. The Luofu is always a busy place filled with people and merchants. I quite like it there.

t_ch: 空气里弥漫的都是利益的味道，哎，堕落啊…我前不久听他们在说，说有个什么巷子里，有个无所不能的当铺，号称什么都收。
t_en: The air reeks of money. Boy, such depravation... Not long ago, I heard the people saying that there's an incredible pawn shop in an alley that buys anything.

t_ch: 真的？能典当爱情吗？我最近附身在一个情种上，天天腻歪得我压根消化不了，还不如趁早卖掉算了。
t_en: Really? Can I pawn off love? Recently, I've been on a hopeless romantic's body, and I'm getting sick of all the lovey-dovey emotions. I would rather trade it off.

t_ch: 我不知道，传说这个巷子的商铺编号是动态变化的，很多人都在找呢。不过要我说，你还是小心些吧，仙舟人个个都是奸商，咱们要是去了肯定都是被吃骨头不吐皮的命。
t_en: I don't know, but rumor has it that the store in the alley changes its location every time, so many people are still searching for it. But my advice to you is to be careful. On the Xianzhou, everyone is an unscrupulous merchant. We'll be skinned alive if we had any.

t_ch: 我寻思我们也没骨头啊？
t_en: I'm confused. Aren't we already dead?

t_ch: 你怎么老缩在学堂里跟小孩子玩，大家都是岁阳，做岁阳得体面些，你就不敢干点正经事吗？
t_en: Why are you always playing with kids at the lyceum? All of us are heliobi, and as heliobi, we need to live with some dignity. Can you get serious for once?

t_ch: 那几个学生最近在测体能，跑步的时候我推着他们跑得飞快，一连破了好几个仙舟记录。我帮人家上学呢，这叫什么玩闹啊，这不算正经事吗？
t_en: These students were having physical tests recently, so I gently nudged them along while they were running to boost their speed. There were even a few who broke the Xianzhou record. Look, I'm not just horsing around but helping these kids in their studies. How isn't this serious?

t_ch: 得了吧，我还不知道你啊，你就贪图人家学生聚在一起够热闹。真没出息，你说你，就知道就学堂里跟着学生瞎混。
t_en: Save the excuses. Do you think I don't know you by now? You just want to join in on the fun. Useless. Look at yourself. You are only wasting your time messing around with these students in class.

t_ch: 才没有！我偶尔也会附身到老师身上，给他们上课，这叫教书育人！那帮小孩子真调皮，老在教室里布下层层机关，想暗算老师一次。还好我够聪明……
t_en: That's not true! I do possess the teacher's body occasionally to teach them. I'm educating and nurturing these people. These kids are playful and love setting traps in the classroom. Luckily, I was smart enough...

t_ch: 看把你能的……
t_en: You can even...

t_ch: …这次我打算换个人附体了。
t_en: ...I decided to possess a different body.

t_ch: 为啥啊，这家人我听你说的，又有钱又懂享受，还喜欢窝在家里，都符合你的喜好啊，不挺好的吗？
t_en: Why? You said the current one suits you since he is rich, loves luxurious life, and enjoys staying home. Isn't that perfect?

t_ch: 哎，这家伙是个持明，身上老有一股味道…我是真受不了了。
t_en: Right, but he's a Vidyadhara. There's always a smell on his body... I can't take it anymore.

t_ch: 这跟是不是持明有什么关系？
t_en: What has that got to do with being a Vidyadhara?

t_ch: 他老惦记着他那古海，天天往浴缸里倒海盐，还说什么这是「大海的味道」。我反正受不了！
t_en: He often misses the ancient sea, so he pours sea salt into his bath daily and claims that it is the "scent of the sea." I had enough!

t_ch: 若是从化外民的角度看，仙舟上的阵法封印几乎都来自太卜司——其实不然，太卜司占了七成，十王司占了两成，鬼画符占了一成。
t_en: From an outworlder's perspective, it almost seems like the matrix seals on Xianzhou are all from the Divination Commission, but that's not true. Only <unbreak>70%</unbreak> of the seals come from the Divination Commission, <unbreak>20%</unbreak> from the Ten-Lords Commission, and the rest are random unintelligible symbols.

t_ch: 你不知道束形却邪阵是否还能正常运转，但你闭上眼，幻想自己身躯膨胀至山丘大小，徒手倒拔起阵中间的柱子的身姿该有多惊人。
t_en: You're not sure if the Evil Binding Matrix is still functioning, but the moment you close your eyes, you imagine your body inflates to the size of a hill. How scary would it be to pluck out the pillar in the middle of the matrix with your bare hands?

t_ch: 根据十王司冥差的说法，曾经有一个岁阳为了报答救命恩人转世持明，闯入人间。却在显出原形之后，被十王司冥差镇伏在镇妖塔中——后来塔被毁掉了。
t_en: According to the spiritfarers of the Ten-Lords Commission, there was a heliobus who reincarnated as a Vidyadhara and entered the human world to repay a benefactor for saving its life. However, once it revealed its true form, a Ten-Lords Commission spiritfarer captured and sealed it in a suppression tower, which was later destroyed.

t_ch: 你应该珍惜这段没有意义的介绍，因为等你镇伏完岁阳之后，这个镇妖塔就不显示调查文本了…真是可惜，见到你是我的荣幸。
t_en: Cherish this meaningless introduction while you still can, because once you've subdued the heliobus, the suppression tower will no longer show you the investigation text... What a shame, it had been an honor to meet you.

t_ch: 不过，其实倒也不必可惜，能用来镇伏岁阳的镇妖塔才是最好的镇妖塔。
t_en: But there's no need for pity, for only the best towers can be used to subdue heliobi.

t_ch: 你试图推开戏台后门，但某种思维惯性掐灭了这刚萌发的念想，你想起品行高尚的人只走正门。
t_en: You try to push open the backstage door, but some kind of ingrained thought extinguishes this budding idea. You remember that a person of a noble character only enters through the front door.

t_ch: 你本想去后台一探究竟，但在你推门时，眼角余光瞥见里面似乎有人在很高的地方沉默眺望这里。你吓了一跳，向后退去，那道高瘦的人影也随之消失。
t_en: You want to explore backstage, but the moment you push open the door, you notice someone silently watching you from a very high place out of the corner of your eyes. Surprised, you leap back, but that slender shadow has vanished.

t_ch: 你本想去后台再探究竟，此时你通过了一次「历史」检定——戏台上的门不应该是这样的，应该是「出将」和「入相」，那么这里究竟是…？
t_en: You originally wanted to explore the backstage further, but at this moment, you pass a History check — doors onstage shouldn't look like this. Shouldn't they be labeled "dextral entrance" and "sinistral exit"? Then what is this place...?

t_ch: 曾经，绥园里有不少传统艺人登台唱戏，被称作「戏子」。时移世易，如今，绥园里星火之精四处横行，它们登上戏台，被称作「戏精」。
t_en: In the past, several traditional performers, known as "drama actors," sang and acted onstage in Fyxestroll Garden. As things change over time, the Starfire Essences are now everywhere in Fyxestroll Garden. Whenever they go on stage, they are known as "drama queens."

t_ch: 别再往门缝里看了，万一真的看到什么可怎么办呀。
t_en: Stop peeping through the doorway. What if you really saw something?

t_ch: 许多年前神秘失踪或沉海的船只，经过许多年后被发现在海上航行，而船里空无一人——它们被叫作「幽灵船」，或者它们只是单纯被认错了。
t_en: Ships that mysteriously disappeared or sank many years ago are found traveling on the sea. Since no one is found on the ship, they are known as "ghost ships," or maybe, they are simply misidentified by the people.

t_ch: 「飞翔的岱舆人号」——传说中永远无法返乡的幽灵船，担负着押送星海中遇难亡灵抵达冥界的使命，注定永远漂泊星海，无法靠岸。
t_en: "Flying Daiyuman" — a legendary ghost ship that can never return home. Its mission is to bring the lost spirits in the sea of stars to the underworld. It is doomed to sail the sea of stars forever, allegedly never able to make port.

t_ch: 在你面前的只是星槎的投影，你明白吗？你不会遇到章鱼头船长，也不会遇到受诅咒的怪物，星槎更不会从海底突然冒出来。
t_en: What's before you is merely the projection of a starskiff, do you understand? You are neither going to meet Captain Octopushead nor the cursed creatures. And the starskiff will not suddenly leap out of the ocean.

t_ch: 书架上弥漫着一股潮湿阴冷的腐朽气息，木纹表面的清漆业已斑驳，但书籍依旧屹立不倒，不禁令人怀疑它们会不会是用来充门面的装饰品。
t_en: A moist and eerie rotten stench from the shelves percolates the air. The varnish on the woodgrain surface has mottled, but the books remain standing on the shelves, making one wonder if they are just for decoration.

t_ch: 乍一看，书籍摆得满满当当，从封面上的灰尘来看，近百年来也没有人翻开过。殊不知，这是十王司带过来的文件…十王司是不是有点跟不上时代了？
t_en: At first glance, the books are packed neatly on the shelves, and judging from the dust on the covers, no one had touched them for nearly a hundred years. Unbeknownst to the others, these are documents brought by the Ten-Lords Commission... Say, aren't they a little behind times?

t_ch: 这也正常，爱书之人总是摆出一副全年阅读清单都排满了的焦急模样，但到最后却连书皮都忘记翻开。
t_en: That's normal. A bibliophile loves getting panicked over a fully packed annual reading list, only to forget to flip open their books at the end.

t_ch: 十王司收集的各类文件——其中不乏某些珍贵的特殊印本，还有特意收集而来的短生种们出版的刊物。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission collects various kinds of documents, including certain rare and valuable special printings, as well as journals published by the short-life species that have been deliberately collected.

t_ch: 你暗自揣测着书卷主人的下落，怀疑对方是否遭遇了某种不测。但知识这东西，生不带来死不带去，想到这里，你不禁松了口气。
t_en: You secretly speculate on the whereabouts of the scroll's owner, wondering if they have met with an unfortunate end. But knowledge isn't a thing that one is born or dies with. You can't help but feel relieved at the thought of it.

t_ch: 看来十王司的「无纸化办公」不行。
t_en: It seems like the Ten-Lords Commission's "paperless work policy" isn't working.

t_ch: 不过十王司要是真的「无纸化办公」了，没有这些像模像样的书架，你会觉得他们是在这里办公吗？不会的。至此，你已经理解了书架的意义。
t_en: But if the Ten-Lords Commission were to go "paperless," do the people here look like they are seriously working without these presentable shelves? Certainly not. At this moment, you suddenly realize the meaning behind the existence of these shelves.

t_ch: 悬浮在夜空中的灯笼，幸福的人看到这星星点点的光芒便会感到温暖。
t_en: With several lanterns floating in the night sky, such a starry view will certainly warm a happy person's heart.

t_ch: 悬在头顶的未必是剑，也可以是灯笼。
t_en: What's hanging above the head may not be a blade. It can also be a lantern.

t_ch: 浮灯微光，并不是只为你而闪耀，有传闻说它身负接引游魂的重责。
t_en: The shimmering light of the floating lanterns does not only shine for you; it is said that it carries the hefty responsibility of guiding wandering souls.

t_ch: 灯笼静静漂浮在夜色中，周身散发着温暖的光芒，平等地为所有人照亮回家的路——尽管并非所有人都有家可归。
t_en: The lanterns silently float in the night sky, each emitting a warm and uniform glow that lights the way home for all, even though not everyone has a home to return to.

t_ch: 灯笼静静漂浮在夜色中，周身散发着温暖的光芒，平等地为所有人照亮回家的路——换句话说，你要是跟着走，不一定会回自己的家。
t_en: The lanterns silently float in the night sky, each emitting a warm glow, equally lighting the way home for all. In other words, you may not necessarily return to your home if you follow the lights.

t_ch: 在某些星球上，曾经有一对父子在暴风雨天气中外出，利用风筝将雷电装进了储存静电的容器之中，他们获得了无数名誉——可是风筝得到了什么。
t_en: On a certain planet, a father-son duo went out on a stormy day and used a kite to contain lightning in a vessel for storing static electricity. As such, they gained a remarkable reputation — but what did the kite gain?

t_ch: 羽翼风筝被遗弃在此，断掉的线上似有斑驳血痕，令你想起在有翼民中曾有悲歌流传——曲中人在恋人死后掷双翼于火中，自此斩绝情爱。
t_en: A feathered kite is abandoned here, and mottled blood stains can be seen on the snapped string, reminding you of a sad song passed among the winged people. The song speaks of how they discard their wings in the fire after the death of their lover as a symbol of detaching from love.

t_ch: 断线的风筝和「无糖星芋啵啵」一样，被人抛弃的命运早已注定。
t_en: A kite with a broken string and a sugar-free Startaro Bubble Tea share the same fate — they are destined to be abandoned.

t_ch: 观察力敏锐的人早就发现了，为逝去的飞行士献上风筝，是狐族人一种不言的传统——可见，飞行早就是他们生命中的一部分了。
t_en: An observant person would have quickly realized that it is an unspoken tradition among the foxians to dedicate kites to fallen pilots — it is evident that flying has long been a part of their lives.

t_ch: 大部分放在这里的风筝应该是用于纪念逝者，少部分是因为风筝线断了，不小心落在绥园里了——不然，你看，树上甚至也挂着有。
t_en: Most of the kites placed here are supposed to be dedicated to the dead, and only a few landed here due to snapped strings. Look, why else do you think those kites are on the trees?

t_ch: 人生的不幸，在碑文中短促的生卒年月里展现得淋漓尽致。
t_en: The short period between birth and death in the inscription vividly shows the misfortunes of one's life.

t_ch: 墓碑上篆刻的字还算清晰可见，但周身遍布裂痕，它的来头或已腐朽。
t_en: The words carved on the tombstone are fairly visible, but the surrounding cracks reveal the poor condition it's in.

t_ch: 一起吃饭的未必是朋友，愿意借钱的也未必是朋友，但愿意勤来对方墓碑前祭拜的，肯定是真心朋友。
t_en: Someone who eats with you or lends you money may not be your friend, but the one who is willing to pay you a visit at your grave is certainly a true friend.

t_ch: 似乎有人在定期维护这块墓碑，墓碑表面虽有风化痕迹，但其余地方却很干净。
t_en: It seems like someone has been regularly maintaining this tombstone. Aside from its weathered surface, the rest of the area is clean and tidy.

t_ch: 在生命消失以后，爱并不会消失，只是换一种方式存在，你对此深信不疑。
t_en: Love doesn't disappear after a life is gone. It just exists in a different way, and you strongly believe in it.

t_ch: 你注视着这块墓碑，碑文上记录着墓主人此生的信条：「谦卑、仁爱、忍耐、节约、勤劳——」
t_en: You gaze at the tombstone, where the inscription records the life creed of the deceased: "Humility, Charity, Patience, Frugality, Diligence—"

t_ch: 你稍移视线，辨认出了墓碑上的最后一句话：「——以上种种我都做不到！」
t_en: You shift your gaze slightly and notice the last sentence on the tombstone: "—All of which I can never attain!"

t_ch: 但至少展现出墓主人的诚实，如今世道，这种品质可不多见了。
t_en: Well, at least it displays the truthfulness of the deceased. It's rare to find honest people these days.

t_ch: 你仔细辨认着墓碑上的字：「抱歉，这次我要断更了。」这似乎是某位小说作者的墓碑。
t_en: You carefully read the inscription on the tombstone: "Sorry, I can't update the story anymore." This seems to be the tombstone of a novel author.

t_ch: 仙舟小说的篇幅之长你早有耳闻，但你相信这位作者的故事并不会就此完结，故事会在读者心间继续连载下去。
t_en: You've heard about the length of Xianzhou's novel, but you believe that this author's story will not end here. The story will continue to unfold in the hearts of readers.

t_ch: 这块墓碑表面的碑文格外不同，原有的碑文已被毁得面目全非，你仔细辨认，只能勉强认出「爱妻」几个字。
t_en: The inscription of this tombstone looks different from the others. The original inscription is now indecipherable. On a closer look, you can only see the words, "my beloved wife."

t_ch: 原有的表面被新篆的碑文所覆盖，内容倒是清晰可见：「她本该有更好的人生。」
t_en: A new inscription is written over the original surface, and the content is clearly visible: "She could have a better life."

t_ch: 你静静地注视着眼前的墓碑，认真阅读着墓碑上篆刻的字迹：
t_en: You silently study the tombstone before you as you solemnly read the inscription carved on it:

t_ch: 「天舶司左侧停泊口办包年有积分，十年清空一次，记得兑换。」看得出来墓主生前是个热心人。
t_en: "The leftmost dock of the Sky-Faring Commission gives points to customers with an annual package. Points are cleared once every decade, so remember to redeem them." You can tell that the deceased was a kind-hearted person.

t_ch: 谢谢你，停泊侠。
t_en: Thank you, Dock Hero.

t_ch: 象征家族的图腾，宣示了五大家系对梦境的统治和维护。
t_en: A totem representing The Family, declaring the Five Lineages' rule and maintenance of the dreamscape.

t_ch: 关于这个图腾所指的家系，来宾们议论纷纷。有人说它头顶的三叶草是苜蓿草家的象征，有人则说它中心的纹路代表了橡木家系。
t_en: Guests have different views of which Family lineage this totem refers to. Some argue that the three-leaf clover at the crown is the symbol of the Alfalfa Family, while others say that the lines in the center represent the Oak Family.

t_ch: 外来者们无法理解的是，匹诺康尼的五大家系真能做到不分彼此，万众如一。图腾的归属于他们而言甚至不如游客的一声喷嚏紧要。
t_en: What outsiders could never understand is that the five major lineages of Penacony are actually one and the same. They represent one large group. The ownership of this totem is as important to them as a guest's sneeze.

t_ch: 阵阵音乐从门后传来，里面或许正在举办一场舞会。
t_en: A chorus of music resounds from behind the door. There may be a ball or something happening inside.

t_ch: 你听见男男女女的笑声彼此交叠…如果你也是其中之一，你会邀请谁呢？
t_en: You can hear the laughter of men and women washing over each other... If you were inside, who would you have invited along?

t_ch: 一对男女的争吵声从门后传来……
t_en: You hear the voices of a man and woman arguing coming from the other side of the door...

t_ch: 早就跟你说过，我们付不起这么高的房费！我们已经没有信用点了！
t_en: I've told you, we can't afford such an expensive room fee! We're out of credits!

t_ch: 怎么会这样…我的美梦，我的美梦还没结束！你赔！
t_en: How could this happen... My dream, my dream is not complete! You have to pay!

t_ch: 别闹了，趁退房时间还没过，赶紧走吧。
t_en: Don't cause a scene. Let's skip the check-out and leave.

t_ch: 房间里只余一片死寂……
t_en: The room falls deadly silent...

t_ch: 什么声音都没有。你尝试将耳朵贴在门板上，细心去察觉。恍神间，你好像听到了枪械上膛的声响，就在房门对面。
t_en: There is no sound at all. You try pressing your ear against the door and listen closely. Suddenly, you feel like you hear the sound of a gun being loaded on the other side of the door.
...Who's there?

t_ch: 声音很虚弱，像是随时会断气那般。并且，你清楚记得你没有敲门。
t_en: Their voice is very weak, like they could take their last breath at any moment. You remember clearly that you never actually knocked on the door.

t_ch: 你好，查水表的。
t_en: Hello, I'm here to check the water meter!

t_ch: 走开…别多管闲事！
t_en: Get lost... This is none of your business!

t_ch: 声嘶力竭的呼喊过后，一切归于平静。
t_en: After the throat-shredding yells, everything fell quiet once more.

t_ch: 房间里没有传来任何声响。
t_en: Not a whisper comes from inside the room.

t_ch: 门后传来噼里啪啦的老式打字机声响，清脆而急促，随后是纸张被狠狠撕碎的声音，每次撕扯仿佛都带着无边的疯狂与恨意。
t_en: From behind the door, you hear the sound of an old-fashioned typewriter, clicking and tapping with a crisp, hurried tempo, followed by the sound of paper being furiously torn to scraps, each tear done with what feels like a frenzied hatred.

t_ch: 紧接着，你听见有个男人反复念叨着什么。
t_en: Shortly after, you hear a man repeating something to themselves.

t_ch: 只工作不玩耍，聪明的孩子也变傻~
t_en: All work and no play make the smart child stupid...

t_ch: 只工作不玩耍，聪明的孩子也变傻~
t_en: All work and no play make the smart child stupid...

t_ch: 一阵慵懒的气息从门后渗出，伴随着似有似无的怨念。
t_en: A sluggish voice resounds from behind the door, carrying a vague sense of resentment.

t_ch: 可是，组长，现在是放假期间……
t_en: But, team leader, it's the holidays...

t_ch: 不，我不是那个意思…您说的对，「一切献给琥珀王」。
t_en: No, that's not what I mean... You're right: "All for the Amber Lord."

t_ch: 是、是吗？太好了……
t_en: Oh, oh really? That's great...

t_ch: 电话挂断的声音传来，随后是身体栽倒在沙发上的声音。
t_en: You hear the sound of the phone being hung up, followed by someone slumping down onto a sofa.

t_ch: 呼，幸好不是真的要加班。
t_en: Phew, so glad I don't have to work overtime.

t_ch: 是时候回到梦中去了…先小睡一会儿吧。
t_en: It's time to return to my dreams... I'll take a little nap.

t_ch: 房门那头传来似有似无的鼾声，那是真实的睡眠。
t_en: You hear what sounds like snoring coming from behind the door. They're sleeping for real.

t_ch: 淋浴的水声始终响着，平稳，空洞，没有变化，没有意义，只是…有点吓人。
t_en: You have heard the sound of a running shower this whole time, steady, empty, unchanging. There is no significance. It's just a little... creepy.

t_ch: ——「不要开门！」不知为何，这样的声音忽然在你的脑海里响起。你很疑惑门后究竟藏着什么，所以你打算……
t_en: "Don't open the door!" You have no idea why, but this voice suddenly rings out in your head. You can't help but wonder what is hidden behind this door, so you decide to...

t_ch: 趴在地上偷窥门缝。
t_en: Lie on the floor and peek through the crack in the door.

t_ch: 轻轻握住房门把手。
t_en: Gently grasp the room door handle.

t_ch: 但你没有任何收获，厚重的房门像是堵无法逾越的高墙，将所有秘密全部吞噬。
t_en: Alas, to no avail. The heavy door is like an insurmountable wall, devouring all secrets within.

t_ch: 你听到门后传来一阵悠扬的提琴声，那动人曲调中似乎饱含思念之情；
t_en: You hear the mellifluous music of violin strings behind the door. This moving song seems to carry a sense of longing;

t_ch: 你听到巨大的敲击声，叮叮咚咚、断断续续，似乎暗藏着某种节律；
t_en: You hear a thunderous, percussive sound from behind the door. Stopping and starting, there seems to be some kind of hidden rhythm;

t_ch: 你听到几声闷响，想象的视野中，子弹正气势汹汹地嵌入昂贵的内墙；
t_en: You hear a number of muffled bangs. In your mind's eye, you imagine bullets voraciously embedding themselves into the interior walls;

t_ch: …继而是死一般的静默。
t_en: ...Followed by a deathly silence.

t_ch: 许久之后，你听见一声叹息——苍老、疲惫，怅然若失。
t_en: After some time, you hear a sigh... They sound old, tired, and dejected.

t_ch: 门后只有一片颓败的死寂。
t_en: You hear nothing behind the door but decadent silence.

t_ch: 这块巨型钟表是盛会之星的地标之一。据说最早由钟表匠制造，后被家族改进为如今的模样。
t_en: This giant clock was a landmark of the Planet of Festivities. It is said that it was originally created by the Watchmaker and later enhanced into its current form by The Family.

t_ch: 虽然名为钟表，但这块大家伙并不真能显示时间——只有抛弃刻度的桎梏，你才能全心全意地享受美梦。
t_en: Though called a "clock," this thing cannot actually display the time... Only by abandoning the shackles of time can one fully enjoy and embrace their dream.

t_ch: 堆满房间的粉红气球，象征着玫瑰般似火的热情。
t_en: Red balloons fill the entire room, symbolizing the fiery passion of roses.

t_ch: 你伸出手、踮起脚，试图从中摘下自己的一束幸福——但它实在太高了，而你又太矮……
t_en: You reach out and stand on your tiptoes, trying to pluck your own little bundle of happiness... But they are too high, and you are too short...

t_ch: 你伸出手、踮起脚，试图从中摘下自己的一束幸福——成功了，你抓住一根气球的尾巴，将它牢牢攥在手中。
t_en: You reach out and stand on your tiptoes, trying to pluck your own little bundle of happiness... Success! You grab the tip of a balloon and grasp it firmly in your hand.

t_ch: 反正这间房里有那么多气球，没人会介意分你一个的，对吧？
t_en: There are countless balloons in this room, after all. Who would begrudge you taking one for yourself, right?

t_ch: 堆满房间的粉红气球，象征着玫瑰般似火的热情。
t_en: Red balloons fill the entire room, symbolizing the fiery passion of roses.

t_ch: 「使用入梦池前，请勿饮用过多苏乐达。」
t_en: "Please do not drink excessive SoulGlad before entering the dreampool."

t_ch: 「想要品尝『元年苏乐达』的味道吗？赶快前往『黄金的时刻』吧！」
t_en: "Do you want to try the taste of Epochal SoulGlad? Hurry toward the Golden Hour!"

t_ch: 「想来一场梦幻般美妙的邂逅吗？『蓝调的时刻』在等着你！」
t_en: "Do you wish for a dreamlike encounter? The Blue Hour awaits!"

t_ch: 飞轮<unbreak>2001</unbreak>，仓鼠骑士同款球笼，通体流线型设计，百公里加速仅耗时<unbreak>2.5</unbreak>秒，助您迅速抵达酒店和梦境的每个角落。详情请咨询「奥帝购物中心」。
t_en: The Flywheel <unbreak>2001</unbreak> is the same Spheroid as the one used by the Hamster Knight. The streamlined design allows the rider to go from 0 to <unbreak>100</unbreak> km/h in <unbreak>2.5</unbreak> seconds, allowing you to reach every corner of the hotel and the dreamscape in seconds. For more information, please contact the Oti Mall.

t_ch: 堆叠的访客行李。即使在去往梦境的途中，有些人也想让自己拿得更多。
t_en: Guest luggage that has been stacked high. Even when visiting the dreamworld, some people want more and more.

t_ch: 有时候，即便是「想要做个美梦」这种事情，也需要你拥有坚实的经济基础。
t_en: Sometimes, even things like "having a beautiful dream" require a solid financial foundation.

t_ch: 能够自动办理入住手续的机器。但由于操作繁琐，许多访客还是更青睐人工通道。
t_en: A machine that can automatically complete the check-in process. However, as it can be a pain to operate, most guests prefer the manual approach.

t_ch: 前面的路被拦住了。作为一个礼貌的无名客，你应该知趣地选择掉头。
t_en: The road ahead is blocked. As a polite Nameless, you should turn back without further prompting.

t_ch: 这条路肯定事出有因才会被拦住…还是遵循酒店的规矩吧。
t_en: This path must be blocked for a reason... Maybe it's best to follow the rules of the hotel.

t_ch: 酒店公用的梦境电话，可以打给不同「时刻」。据说拨号盘上的眼睛会在通话时活过来。
t_en: A hotel public dreamphone that can make calls to different Moments. It is said that the eyes on the phone dial come alive during a call.

t_ch: 存放来宾贵重物品的金库，整个酒店里最安全的地方。考虑到大堂氛围不该太过严肃，设计师在它的门上额外安装了一张唱片。
t_en: The vault that stores all of the VIP guests' most valuable belongings, the safest place in the entire hotel. Considering that they did not want too stern an atmosphere in the lobby, the designer installed a record around the door.

t_ch: 酒店的金库门，边缘是一张巨大的唱片。倘若大堂的音乐停止，便是有人要取出极其贵重的东西。
t_en: The door to the hotel vault, bordered with a huge record. If the lobby music stops, it means that someone is going to try and remove something extremely valuable.

t_ch: 《在夜幕下与楼同奏》
t_en: Tower Rhapsody Under the Moonlight

t_ch: 《漏斗与花朵碰杯》
t_en: A Cheers of Funnel and Flower

t_ch: 本店不提供任何酒精饮料。
t_en: This hotel does not provide any alcoholic beverages.

t_ch: 元年苏乐达——一个笑话。
t_en: Epochal SoulGlad — a joke.

t_ch: 信用点和苜蓿币皆可支付。
t_en: Credits and Alfalfa credits accepted.

t_ch: 随机赠送饮品——如果店长心情好的话。
t_en: Receive a random drink — if the hotel manager is in a good mood.

t_ch: 高脚杯中没有一滴酒，全是苏乐达。
t_en: There's not a drop of alcohol in the goblet. It's all SoulGlad.

t_ch: 一杯饮品附赠一个小秘密，口味配方随您挑选。
t_en: Each glass comes with a little secret — you get to pick the flavor combination!

t_ch: 这台电梯通往酒店数之不尽的其他楼层，不过现在你并不想去。
t_en: This elevator goes to the endless other floors of the hotel, but you have no desire to leave right now.

t_ch: 你确定还要在这枯燥无味的现实里浪费时间，而不是及早投身美梦中的狂欢？
t_en: Are you sure you want to continue wasting time in this dry and flavorless reality? Why not cast yourself into the carnival within a beautiful dream?

t_ch: 酒店房间标配的恒温装置。其火焰可以在吸收热量和释放热量间切换，从而使房间温度下降或上升。
t_en: Thermostats are fitted into the hotel rooms as standard. Their flames can be switched to either absorb or release heat, heating or cooling the room.

t_ch: 人生最美妙的享受是什么？当然是一边听音乐，一边喝苏乐达。而这台设备可以让你同时做到两者。
t_en: What is the most precious moment in life? Listening to music while enjoying SoulGlad, of course! This device right here lets you enjoy both.

t_ch: 客房的内线电话，可以向酒店要求各种服务。
t_en: An internal hotel phone. You can request various services from the hotel staff.

t_ch: 据说一些高级客户能够拥有自己的专线，仅需动动指头就能召来七八位侍者。
t_en: Apparently, certain high-paying customers can receive their own personal lines, and can call up half a dozen servants with just a twitch of their finger.

t_ch: 《梦境的数种拟像》
t_en: Dreamscape: A Catalog of Simulacra

t_ch: 并未完全拆封的礼物，堆在这里等待着有资格打开它们的人。
t_en: Gifts that have not been fully opened are stacked here, waiting for those qualified to open them.

t_ch: 有人说所有礼物背后都已经标好了价格。对这间房而言，价格或许是「浪漫」。
t_en: People often say that there is a price behind every gift. When it comes to this room, perhaps the price is "romance."

t_ch: 堆了半墙的书籍和影像带，其中有不少是匹诺康尼的古早出品。
t_en: Books and tapes are stacked halfway up the wall. Among them are a considerable number of early Penacony works.

t_ch: 在梦境内容如此丰富的当下，书籍这样原始的载体只能起到纪念意义了。
t_en: At present, where dreams are as abundant as they are, primitive items like books serve only as commemorative objects.

t_ch: 比起枯燥地探索完酒店的每一层，还是回到梦中更有趣些。
t_en: Returning to dreaming would be far more entertaining than merely searching every single floor of the hotel.

t_ch: 你担心电梯门后钻出一对双胞胎来。
t_en: You're worried a pair of terrifying twins are going to emerge from behind the elevator doors.

t_ch: 人不能两次踏入一台相同的电梯，也不能一次乘坐两台电梯。
t_en: You cannot step into the same elevator twice, just as you cannot take two elevators at once.

t_ch: 这样的门连接着一间间侧厅——酒店的五脏六腑。但梦境显然比它们更值得开拓。
t_en: Doors like this lead to one side room after another — the heart and soul of the hotel. However, the dreamscape is clearly much more worth exploring.

t_ch: 紧闭的门并不会断绝探索的可能——它们终将开启，只要你耐心等待。
t_en: A firmly closed door does not mean that exploration is impossible... They have to open sometimes, you just need to be patient.

t_ch: 这座酒店为宾客们准备了许多现实娱乐，但它们都不如梦境有吸引力。
t_en: This hotel has prepared a lot of real-life entertainment, but they are not as attractive as entering the dreamscape.

t_ch: 这处花坛里种的是三日卷柏——闻名宇宙的复苏植物。
t_en: This flower bed has three-day spikemosses, known across the universe as the plant of resurrection.

t_ch: 据说在非自然死亡时，它只需阳光照射三天就能起死回生，因此也被戏称作「宇宙中最好养活的盆栽」。
t_en: Legend has it that in the case of unnatural death, it only requires three days of sunshine to be able to bring the dead back to life. That is why it has been nicknamed: "The easiest plant to raise in the universe."

t_ch: 不过由于缺少阳光，这一坛的长势只能算凑合。
t_en: However, due to the lack of sunlight, the growth of this pot can only be considered passable.

t_ch: 这是一台自助苏乐达机，在匹诺康尼随处可见。
t_en: This is an automatic SoulGlad dispenser machine, common in Penacony.

t_ch: 当你靠近时，这台机器上的加液枪突然掉落，喷出的苏乐达溅了你一身。
t_en: As you approach, the dispenser on the machine suddenly falls, spraying SoulGlad all over you.

t_ch: 一个令人恼怒的恶作剧——也可能单纯是一场意外，没人知道。
t_en: An infuriating prank — or it could simply be an accident. Who knows.

t_ch: 你四下张望，却找不到一个可以抱怨的对象……
t_en: You look around, but can't find anyone to vent to...

t_ch: 这是…一个垃圾桶。梦中居然也存在垃圾桶？！
t_en: This is... a trashcan. There are even trashcans in the dream!?

t_ch: 这一发现化作巨大的喜悦，转瞬就冲上了你的脑壳，仿佛一记重拳。
t_en: This discovery turns into overwhelming joy, and it rushes to your head like a punch.

t_ch: 等回过神来时，你整个人都已经包裹在了糖色的情绪之中，带着些许回甘。
t_en: When you return to your senses, you've been enveloped in a sugary mood, with a lingering sweet aftertaste.

t_ch: 你把眼睛凑到望远镜前，凝视其中的景象——只看到一片深渊般的漆黑。
t_en: You peer into the binoculars, but all that meets your gaze is an abyssal darkness.

t_ch: 突然，深渊中睁开了一只眼睛，也开始凝视着你……
t_en: Suddenly, an eye opens in the abyss and begins to stare at you...

t_ch: 继续凝视它。
t_en: Continue to meet its gaze.

t_ch: 凝视深渊之人，必将被深渊凝视。而你选择与深渊对视……
t_en: If you gaze into the abyss, the abyss gazes also into you. And if you choose to meet the abyss' gaze...

t_ch: 不知过了多久，那只眼睛羞涩地合上了。望远镜中只留一片黑暗。
t_en: After some time, the eye closes shyly. Only darkness remains in the telescope.

t_ch: 移开视线。
t_en: Avert your gaze.

t_ch: 你移开视线，不再与望远镜大眼瞪小眼。
t_en: You avert your gaze, no longer meeting the stare of the telescope.

t_ch: 靠近这扇门时，你听到里面传来了女人的尖锐嗓音。
t_en: As you approach the door, you hear a woman's shrill voice coming from within.

t_ch: 你一开始怎么不告诉我？出了事你担得起责任吗！
t_en: Why didn't you tell me from the start? Can you take the blame if something happens?

t_ch: 可梦境的宣传语不是说，「做你想做的任何人」吗？
t_en: But isn't the slogan of the Dreamscape "Be whoever you want to be"?

t_ch: 那也不代表你可以在偶像直播中搓脚皮！
t_en: But that doesn't mean you're free to rub the skin from your feet during the live stream!

t_ch: 斯坦威游隼，加长型豪华飞车。运行平稳，乘坐舒适，在梦境中使用苏乐达动力。成功人士的品质之选。
t_en: The Steinway Falcon is a sleek and luxurious speedster. Smooth and comfortable, it's powered by SoulGlad in the Dreamscape. It is a choice of quality for the successful.

t_ch: 即便在群星云集的匹诺康尼，一辆斯坦威游隼也会是彰显您高贵品位的最好标识。
t_en: Even amidst the star-studded Penacony, a Steinway Falcon is the epitome of showcasing your class.

t_ch: 斯坦威游隼，原产地匹诺康尼，以「美丽」之称闻名遐迩——既包括它美丽的外形，也包括它美丽的售价。
t_en: The Steinway Falcon, originating from Penacony, is renowned for its beauty — both in terms of its appearance and price.

t_ch: 随处可见的自助苏乐达机，拔管即饮。也可以为交通工具添加动力。
t_en: Self-service SoulGlad machines are seen anywhere. Simply pull out the tube and drink. It can also be used to power your vehicles.

t_ch: 对于苏乐达而言，经典的永远是最好的。
t_en: For SoulGlad, classics are always the best.

t_ch: 本机新上「梦境蓝环乌贼与草木灰」口味苏乐达，限时八折优惠，欲购从速！
t_en: The new SoulGlad flavor, "Dreamscape Blue Ring Squid & Plant Ash," is now available from this machine at a <unbreak>20%</unbreak> discount for a limited time only!

t_ch: 黄金时刻随处可见的文化杆，化用了匹诺康尼第一人气IP钟表小子的形象。没有指路和报时功能。
t_en: The cultural poles that are all over the Golden Hour are made after the image of Penacony's top intellectual property, Clockie. It does not provide directions or tell the time.

t_ch: 据说在漆黑环境下，这些装饰牌也会发出光芒——但「黄金的时刻」总是灯火通明，根本没机会用到它们。
t_en: They are said to glow in the dark — but the Golden Hour is always well-lit, so there's no chance of using them.

t_ch: 「我在匹诺康尼很想你」——最下方的牌子上刻着这样一行小字。
t_en: "I am missing you in Penacony." A small line is inscribed at the bottom of the plaque.

t_ch: 右转：艾迪恩公园；后退：格拉克斯大道。
t_en: Turn right: Aideen Park; Go back: Glaux Avenue.

t_ch: 左转：艾迪恩公园；后退：钟表小子广场。
t_en: Turn left: Aideen Park; Go back: Clockie Plaza.

t_ch: 直行：奥帝购物中心；后退：钟表小子广场。
t_en: Go straight: Oti Mall; Go back: Clockie Plaza.

t_ch: 直行：奥帝购物中心；后退：格拉克斯大道。
t_en: Go straight: Oti Mall; Go back: Glaux Avenue.

t_ch: 「黄金的时刻」随处可见的电话亭，据说可以和梦境之外联络。但眼前这座暂时无法使用。
t_en: A telephone booth that is commonly found in the Golden Hour. They're said to be able to communicate with people outside the Dreamscape, but this one is currently out of order.

t_ch: 据说梦境中的许多物品都有自己的心智，眼前这座电话亭或许是不想让你进去。
t_en: It's rumored that many objects in the Dreamscape possess minds of their own. Perhaps this particular phone booth before you does not want you to enter it.

t_ch: 或许你需要满足一些条件才能使用这座电话亭，比如装一条机械臂、纹个纹身、或者养几只宠物什么的。
t_en: Maybe you need to fulfill certain criteria to use this phone booth. Try installing a robotic arm, getting a tattoo, or raising a few pets.

t_ch: 梦境中的垃圾桶。除了常规垃圾外，这里还存放着人们废弃的情绪。
t_en: A trashcan in the Dreamscape. Other than the usual trash, it is also a location for people's discarded emotions.

t_ch: 就像开盲盒——你永远也不会知道，在打开一个垃圾桶时，里面的房客此时究竟是何种心情。
t_en: It's like opening a loot box. You'll never know how the tenant inside is feeling when you open the trashcan.

t_ch: 据说在梦里就连垃圾都有喜怒哀乐，这让你不由得开始思考，我们同这些垃圾之间，究竟有何区别。
t_en: It's rumored that even trash in the Dreamscape has feelings. It makes you wonder: How are we different from mere trash?

t_ch: 打包好的垃圾，会在来宾看不到的角落统一销毁。
t_en: Packaged trash will be disposed of, out of sight of the guests.

t_ch: 这袋子里不知装着多少人的精神垃圾。
t_en: Who knows how many people's mental trash is in this bag.

t_ch: 梦境特制的灭火设施——里面装的可不是苏乐达。
t_en: The Dreamscape's specialized fire extinguisher. It's not filled with SoulGlad.

t_ch: 据传某男子曾蓄意破坏消防栓，只为喝到「更猛烈」的苏乐达。他离开梦境的姿势不算体面。
t_en: Rumor has it that a man deliberately destroyed a fire hydrant just to get his hands on a "stronger" SoulGlad. He didn't leave the Dreamscape with his dignity intact.

t_ch: 消防栓，内无垃圾。
t_en: Fire hydrant. No trash inside.

t_ch: 你不知道这些杂物箱里究竟装着什么，但肯定不会是爱和希望。
t_en: You don't know what exactly is within the box, but it definitely won't be love and hope.

t_ch: 即便是在梦里，这堆箱子之间也绝不可能藏着一只宝箱怪…大概吧？
t_en: Even in a dream, there's no way a treasure chest monster can be hiding between these boxes... or is there?

t_ch: 供游客欣赏梦境时刻壮丽景象的望远镜。不过这架望远镜的视线十分模糊，让你感觉像是在顶着一个桶看东西。
t_en: A telescope for tourists to enjoy the splendid views of the Dreamscape. However, the view is blurry, and it feels like you're looking through a barrel.

t_ch: 供游客欣赏梦境时刻壮丽景象的望远镜。不过这架望远镜里面被堵住了，你只能看见一片漆黑。
t_en: A telescope for tourists to enjoy the splendid views of the Dreamscape. However, the view is blocked, so all you can see is darkness.

t_ch: …不知道为什么，这个望远镜里面正在放映「钟表小子」的系列动画。
t_en: ...For some reason, this telescope is showing a Clockie serial animation.

t_ch: 在这个望远镜里，你看见了浩渺群星，过去、现在与未来正交织为壮丽的图景——唯独没有对面的高楼。
t_en: In this telescope, you can see the stars, the past, the present, and the future — all weaved into a magnificent picture. You can see everything but the skyscrapers on the other side.

t_ch: 你凑近望远镜，未曾想城市的灯火昏花了你的眼睛，你感到心神缭乱、头晕目眩…你终于明白——这叫光污染。
t_en: You get close to the binoculars, not expecting the city lights to be so blinding. You feel dizzy and lightheaded... You finally understand — this is light pollution.

t_ch: 「海珍达兹冰淇淋——梦境的味道，就是这么鲜明。」
t_en: "Hygen Daaz Ice Cream — This is how distinct the taste of the Dreamscape is."

t_ch: 「最美味的美梦脆筒，就在海珍达兹。」
t_en: "The most delectable Dreamy Cone is here at Hygen Daaz."

t_ch: 「苏乐达好处都有啥，谁说中了就送他。」
t_en: "What are the benefits of SoulGlad? Whoever gets it right wins this!"

t_ch: 「苏乐达——欢乐的源泉、美梦的精华！」
t_en: "SoulGlad — the source of joy, the quintessence of dreams!"

t_ch: 金黄色的饮料如火山般喷发——苏乐达似乎非常喜欢这个宣传设计，就连它们的工厂也是这幅造型。
t_en: The golden drink erupts like a volcano. SoulGlad seems to love this advertising design, and even their factory is designed this way.

t_ch: 「乌通蓬自动车，成功人士的豪华尊享。」
t_en: "Utopon Motion Maverick, luxury indulgence for the successful."

t_ch: 你无法想象这块广告牌多么渴望像球笼一般自由。
t_en: You can't imagine how much this billboard wants to be free as a spheroid.

t_ch: 「柠檬汁、红鱼子，酸甜可口、老少皆宜。」
t_en: "Lemon juice, fish roe, sweet and sour for all ages."

t_ch: 曾几何时，「绯鱼先生」柠檬挞里真的有过鲱鱼。
t_en: Once upon a time, Mr. Herring's lemon tarts really did contain herrings.

t_ch: 「零卡圣代，美味无负担。」
t_en: "Zero-calorie sundaes. Delicious and guilt-free."

t_ch: 飞碟堡，匹诺康尼颇负盛名的美食之一。据说某些酒馆里正在举办「不用手吃飞碟堡」的比赛。
t_en: Floatdisc Burger, one of Penacony's most famous delicacies. Rumor has it that some taverns are hosting a "Hands-free Floatdisc Burger Eating Contest."

t_ch: 「衣装是邂逅浪漫的武器，而你值得最好的。」
t_en: "Clothing is the best weapon for encountering romance, and you're worth the best clothes."

t_ch: 「我们不只妆点您的外貌，还能修饰您的灵魂。」
t_en: "We don't just beautify your face, but also rejuvenate your soul."

t_ch: 「今天的美好梦境，明天的难忘回忆。」
t_en: "Today's Dreamscape, tomorrow's memories."

t_ch: 「潮流时尚，精品典藏——尽在于此！」
t_en: "Chic, trendy, and a fine collection — it's all here."

t_ch: 「钟表披萨，钟表小子都爱吃的聚会美食。」
t_en: "Clockie Pizza. A party food that even Clockie loves."

t_ch: 「条条大路织美梦，往来交通有球笼。」
t_en: "All roads lead to dreams, all travels use Spheroids."

t_ch: 对于苏乐达而言，经典的永远是最好的。
t_en: For SoulGlad, classics are always the best.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼常见的自助推车，主打一个方便快捷、数量繁多。
t_en: A self-serve goods trolley. It is a common sight in Penacony with its convenience, speed of service, and ubiquitousness.

t_ch: 苏乐达是梦境中最为常见的饮料——当然，还是得花钱购买。
t_en: SoulGlad is the most common dream in the Dreamscape — you still have to pay for it though.

t_ch: 这辆推车的主人不知去哪里享受了，只留冰淇淋孤零零地立在架子上——好在梦中的冰淇淋不会融化。
t_en: You're not sure where the owner of this cart ran off to relax, leaving the ice cream on the shelves all sad and alone. Luckily, ice cream doesn't melt in dreams.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼常见的自助推车，主打一个方便快捷、数量繁多。
t_en: A self-serve goods trolley. It is a common sight in Penacony with its convenience, speed of service, and ubiquitousness.

t_ch: 这辆推车上摆放着成捆的报纸，任游客们自行拿取——当心别挑中那些过期的。
t_en: This cart is filled with newspapers, free for grabs for tourists. Be careful not to pick the outdated ones.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼常见的自助推车，主打一个方便快捷、数量繁多。
t_en: A self-serve goods trolley. It is a common sight in Penacony with its convenience, speed of service, and ubiquitousness.

t_ch: 啪砰——那是快乐绽放的声响。
t_en: Pop — this is the sound of happiness.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼街边的自助餐车。不知为何总有人试图闯进车厢，看看里面究竟有什么。
t_en: A roadside self-serve food truck in Penacony. For some reason, people keep trying to barge into the car to see what is inside.

t_ch: 在匹诺康尼，不管要去哪里，都可以手捧一桶爆米花以作消遣。
t_en: No matter where you go in Penacony, you can hold a bucket of popcorn as a pastime.

t_ch: 门口贴着这样的标语：「再强调一次，这里不提供打手服务！！」
t_en: A sign on the door reads: "To emphasize, we do not provide thug-for-hire services!!"

t_ch: 匹诺康尼街边的自助餐车。不知为何总有人试图闯进车厢，看看里面究竟有什么。
t_en: A roadside self-serve food truck in Penacony. For some reason, people keep trying to barge into the car to see what is inside.

t_ch: 钟表小子专属周边贩售店，据说某些纪念品的材料直接取自钟表小子雕像。
t_en: Exclusive Clockie merchandise shop. Rumor has it that certain merch are made from materials directly harvested from the Clockie Statue.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼街边的自助商车。不知为何总有人试图闯进车厢，看看里面究竟有什么。
t_en: A roadside self-serve shopping truck in Penacony. For some reason, people keep trying to barge into the car to see what is inside.

t_ch: 各色商品在灯光下闪闪发亮，透过橱窗，你看见了精心包裹的欲望。
t_en: Various bits of merchandise glimmer under the light. Through the window, you see meticulously wrapped desire.

t_ch: 这家店铺卖的东西和对门完全相同，其中竞争想必非常激烈。
t_en: This store sells the same exact merchandise as the one opposite it. The competition must be fierce between the two.

t_ch: 这橱窗让你回忆起某个童话故事——你不禁开始想象，要是点燃一根火柴、或者捏碎一个梦泡，里面的东西会不会活过来。
t_en: This display sparks your memory of a fairy tale. You cannot help but imagine — what if you light a match, or crush a dream bubble? Will the things inside come to life?

t_ch: 音乐，欢笑，咖啡的清香，杯盏轻碰的脆响…哪怕不曾亲眼所见，你也能猜出门后的景象是多么有格调。
t_en: Music, laughter, the aroma of coffee, and the crisp clinking of cups... Even if not seen by your own eyes, it is not difficult to imagine the elegant scene behind the doors.

t_ch: 平平无奇的面包房，却是能给所有人带来幸福和满足的心灵港湾。
t_en: An ordinary bakery. Yet, it is a haven that brings happiness and warmth to everyone's souls.

t_ch: 即便隔着橱窗，你也能看到香甜、嗅到柔软、品尝到色泽金黄。
t_en: Even through the display windows, you can see the sweetness, smell the softness, and taste the golden hue.

t_ch: 面包，最适合果腹的食品之一。它在梦里远比现实中香甜许多。
t_en: Bread, one of the most suitable food to fill the stomach. It is even more delicious in the dream than in reality.

t_ch: 这家店铺贩卖的东西弥足珍贵，唯有数目庞大的金钱才能衡量。
t_en: This store sells items so precious that they can only be valued with a large sum of money.

t_ch: 奢侈品是种奇妙的东西：它们身价高昂，却从不拒绝被人拥有。
t_en: Luxury goods are truly wonderful. They carry a hefty price tag, but are still willing to be owned by people.

t_ch: 出奇豪华的餐厅。在梦中用餐不会有饱腹感，却能完整体验食物的美味——真是一举两得。
t_en: An astoundingly luxurious restaurant. You'll never feel full when you eat in the dream, yet you can fully experience the flavors of food — it kills two birds with one stone.

t_ch: 幽鲁士餐饮连锁的分店之一，家庭宴会和朋友小聚的不二选择。
t_en: One of Yuluxe's Culinary Chain's branches. It's the top choice for family and friend gatherings.

t_ch: 链接梦境与现实的银行。当口袋里的钞票见底时，你可以拜托他们帮你从现实取些过来……
t_en: A bank that is both connected to dream and reality. When your pockets run out of money, you can ask them to transfer money from reality...

t_ch: 不是所有来宾都能用到这项服务——因为大家在现实里也未必有钱。
t_en: Not all guests are able to use this service — as not everyone has money in reality.

t_ch: 报社，出产新闻，产出真相。
t_en: A newspaper publisher. They produce news and disseminate the truth.

t_ch: 每个梦境时刻都有专门负责传播好事的报社，此为其中之一。
t_en: Every Dreamscape Moment has a newspaper publisher dedicated to spreading good news — this is one of them.

t_ch: 挂在门口的标语：我们制造真理。
t_en: A slogan hangs on the door: We create the truth.

t_ch: 设立在桥下的店铺，远离大众视线，又不过分偏僻——是秘密集会的绝佳地点。
t_en: A store under the bridge, far from public eyes, but not too obscure. A perfect place for secret gatherings.

t_ch: 门后似乎是某个高档会所，玻璃的防窥探效果很好，在街上看不出太多端倪。
t_en: Behind the door seems to be some kind of upscale club. The glass is coated with a security layer that prevents prying eyes. You can't see much of it from the street.

t_ch: 「人靠衣装车靠胎。」只要走进这家店，丑小鸭也能变成黑天鹅。
t_en: "Clothes make the man and tires make the car." If you walk into this store, even an ugly duckling can also transform into a swan.

t_ch: 橱窗里琳琅满目的服饰预示了这家店铺不菲的消费。
t_en: The displays are full of dazzling clothes that imply the extravagant prices in the store.

t_ch: 鸢尾花家系旗下的娱乐工作室，无数稚嫩梦想的起点与终点。
t_en: The entertainment studio under the Iris Family — the beginning and end of many naive dreams.

t_ch: 知更鸟的故事鼓舞了许多人，让他们以为自己能像雏鸟一样飞翔——事实证明，雏鸟总是会跌落的。
t_en: Robin's story has inspired many, making them think that they could soar into the sky as a nestling... When in fact a baby bird will surely fall to the ground.

t_ch: 为普通人提供服务的苏乐达俱乐部。相比于饮料本身，「邂逅」才是这里更珍贵的商品。
t_en: A SoulGlad club that provides services for ordinary people. Even more than the beverage itself, "encounters" are a much more precious commodity here.

t_ch: 一家专供人类的苏乐达俱乐部。里面的顾客身高正常，没有尾巴或长耳，身体也不是铁皮构成的。
t_en: A SoulGlad club that primarily serves humans. Its customers are of usual height, have no tails nor long ears, and are not constructed from steel.

t_ch: 身高<unbreak>1.3</unbreak>米以上不得入内！
t_en: No entry for individuals over <unbreak>1.3</unbreak>m tall!

t_ch: 会所内免费供应苏乐达——再强调一遍，这里都是成年人！
t_en: SoulGlad is provided at no charge in the club — I repeat, only adults around here!

t_ch: 专供皮皮西的苏乐达俱乐部，不过它的门对皮皮西来说有些太高了。
t_en: A SoulGlad club that primarily serves Pepeshi, though the door is way too tall for them.

t_ch: 这是一家外貌调整店，可以让你像某些游戏的开头一样随心所欲地修改形象——当然，仅限梦中。
t_en: This is an appearance adjustment shop that allows you to tweak your looks any way you want, just like at the beginning of certain games... Only effective in the dream, of course.

t_ch: 「…为了让您回到现实后不会对自己的外观感到失望，我们贴心地关闭了『外貌调整』功能。」
t_en: ...To ensure you do not feel disappointed in your looks when you return to reality, we have kindly turned off the Appearance Adjustment function.

t_ch: 这是一家「再次体验店」，可以让你重温梦境中最难忘的经历。
t_en: This is a Re-Experience shop, where you can relive your most memorable experiences in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 梦中总有一些经历让你没齿难忘，这家店铺旨在帮助人们像复习书本一样重温那份心动。
t_en: Surely there are some episodes that you simply could not forget in your dream — this shop helps people re-live those tender emotions as if they were revising a book.

t_ch: 所有城市都需要一座酒店来见证历史，「黄金的时刻」也不例外。
t_en: All cities need a hotel to witness history, and the Golden Hour is no exception.

t_ch: 如果你想在梦中享受豪华的酒店套房，这会是你的不二之选。
t_en: If you wish to enjoy a luxurious hotel suite in your dream, there is no better choice.

t_ch: 不同于「白日梦」酒店，这家酒店的套房内既没有入梦池、也没有床。
t_en: Different from The Reverie, there are no Dreampool nor beds in this hotel's suite.

t_ch: 得体的客人们围坐在玻璃窗后的圆桌前。当需要谈论秘密时，他们便会拉起窗帘——看来今天的秘密有点多。
t_en: Respectable guests sit before the round table behind the glass. When they must discuss secrets, they will pull down the curtain. It appears there are many secrets today.

t_ch: 装修漂亮的店往往意味着里面的商品价格不菲，看来大部分人注定与这里无缘。
t_en: A shop with gorgeous decor usually suggests that its prices are quite dear — it looks like most people are not destined to enter this place.

t_ch: 厄皮洛斯服装秀场的分店，仅为VIP会员提供服务。店面不大，恰好能容下那些口袋阔绰的贵宾。
t_en: Branch outlet of the Epirus Apparel Fashion Show that only caters to its VIP members. The shop isn't spacious — just enough to host distinguished customers with deep pockets.

t_ch: 作为匹诺康尼的文化符号，苏乐达几乎可以出现在任何店铺的招牌上，而无需与商品关联——就比如这其实是一家潮玩店。
t_en: As a cultural symbol of Penacony, SoulGlad can show up on almost any store's sign without any association with its products. For example, this shop is actually a pop gadget store.

t_ch: 一家潮玩店，找不到标识，也没有任何宣传招牌。
t_en: A pop gadget where no logo can be found, and is devoid of any promotional signs.

t_ch: 这是一家典当铺，可用珍贵的物品或情绪作抵押。梦中总是有人需要这类服务。
t_en: This is a pawnshop where precious items or emotions can be used as collateral. There will always be people who need such services in the dream.

t_ch: 这家店铺不售卖商品——它只负责把珍贵之物换成钱财，好让那些不愿离开的穷光蛋在梦中多待些时日。
t_en: This shop does not sell items — it is only responsible for trading valuable items for money, allowing the poor who do not wish to leave to spend a few more days in the dream.

t_ch: 人类、皮皮西、智械…这座大型俱乐部欢迎每一位喜爱苏乐达的宾客。这就是匹诺康尼的精神——来者不拒。
t_en: Humans, Pepeshi, Intellitrons... This massive club welcomes every guest who loves SoulGlad. This is the spirit of Penacony — All are welcome.

t_ch: 格拉克斯大街上最具规模的苏乐达俱乐部，足以接待每一位奇形怪状的宾客。
t_en: The largest SoulGlad club along Glaux Avenue, enough to host every odd and eccentric customer that comes through its doors.

t_ch: 苏乐达俱乐部的后门，一些注重私密性的顾客会从这里悄悄进出。
t_en: The SoulGlad Club's back door. Customers who value their privacy will enter or slip out through this exit.

t_ch: 有时这扇门会迎接一些着装不算体面的顾客，有时它会用来销毁秘密。
t_en: This door is sometimes used to welcome more unsavory customers. Other times, it is used to destroy secrets.

t_ch: 当你靠近这扇门时，一段旋律突然从门后传来……
t_en: As you near this door, a melody can suddenly be heard from behind it...

t_ch: 你顿时感觉像被微风吹拂。空气中泛着咖啡的焦香，令你嘴里生涎。
t_en: You instantly feel like you're being blown at by a breeze. The air is tinged with a faint burnt scent of coffee, making your mouth salivate.

t_ch: 柜台上的表盘躁动起来，指针开始飞速旋转，仿佛要把你带到遥远过去的某个时刻……
t_en: The dial on the counter is stirred into life. Its needle starts to spin quickly as if it is about to take you to a distant moment in the past...

t_ch: 「为什么，为什么事情会变成这样……」
t_en: Why? Why has it come to this?

t_ch: 「冷静…现在只有我了…必须完成他留下的嘱托…我必须做到……」
t_en: "Calm down... It's just me left now... I have to complete the instructions he left behind... I have to do it..."

t_ch: 「可是…我该怎么做到…我怎么可能做到？」
t_en: "But... how do I do it... How can I possibly complete it?"

t_ch: 「唉…难道没人能拉我一把吗？」
t_en: "*sigh*... Is there no one who can help me?"

t_ch: 靠近这面书架时，你闻到了令人平静的木头气息——不是活的，而是原生的、经过碾压和漂白的木制纸张。
t_en: When you near this bookshelf, you catch a whiff of a wood scent that soothes the nerves — not alive, but all-natural, compressed, and bleached paper made from wood.

t_ch: 即便以梦境的尺度来看，这里的藏书数量也堪称夸张，仿佛过往历史的丰碑。
t_en: Even by Dreamscape measurements, the number of books stashed here border on hyperbole, as if monuments to history.

t_ch: 在万千书籍的注视下，你心底萌发出了一股愉悦的暖流，令你毛孔大开……
t_en: Under the gaze of these tens of thousands of books, a warm and joyful stream radiates from your inner self, making your pores expand...

t_ch: 随着你的靠近，积木们纷纷躁动起来。它们发出声音，如同一千张嘴同时开口……
t_en: As you get closer, the densely packed blocks start to stir. They make a sound akin to a thousand mouths opening at once...

t_ch: 「看啊，我们坚固如山。再激烈的玩闹也不足以让我们倒塌。」
t_en: "Look here, we are immovable like a mountain. Even the most intense shakedowns will not topple us."

t_ch: 「可是没了玩伴，这一切还有什么意义？」
t_en: "But what's the point of everything if we have no playmates?"

t_ch: 「不会回来了…他被遗弃了，所以我们也被抛下……」
t_en: "He won't return... He has been discarded, and so have we..."

t_ch: 隐约间，你似乎听到积木们在低声啜泣。但当你想要仔细去听时，一切声音都消失了。
t_en: Very faintly, you seem to hear the blocks whimpering softly. But when you want to listen more closely, all sound has vanished.

t_ch: 柔软的沙发。上面可能承载过某些柔软的记忆。
t_en: A very soft sofa. Many soft memories may have been borne on it.

t_ch: 表皮光滑而完整，可以肯定的是，房间的主人绝对没有在这养过宠物。
t_en: Its surface is glossy and flawless. It is most certain that the master of this room never kept pets.

t_ch: 舒适无比的沙发，一不留神就会让你浪费数个小时。
t_en: An extremely comfy sofa. You'll waste many hours lounging on it if you're not paying attention.

t_ch: 你随手抽出本书、翻开，里面的字符模糊成一片，即便是联觉信标也识别不出来。
t_en: You casually pull out a book and flip it open — the characters in it are so smudged that even a Synesthesia Beacon cannot comprehend them.

t_ch: 你试图拿起旁边的笔，在纸上留下几句记录，却怎么也写不出能辨认的文字。
t_en: You try to pick up a nearby pen to write a few notes on the paper but realize that you can't even write in any legible language.

t_ch: 你看完了书，却怎么也记不住它的内容——据说人从梦中醒来后也是如此。
t_en: You finish reading the book, but somehow can't remember its contents. It's said that this phenomenon is the same for someone who has just woken up from a dream.

t_ch: 两人份的下午茶——会是谁呢？现在他们又在哪里？
t_en: Afternoon tea for two — who could they have been? Where have they gone?

t_ch: 桌上的茶杯空空如也，里面的液体已经干涸。看来这里已经荒废了很长时间。
t_en: The teacups on the table are empty, the liquid inside long dried. Looks like they have been abandoned for a long time.

t_ch: 仙舟有个成语「人走茶凉」，这里就是它的绝佳应用场景。
t_en: The Xianzhou has a saying: The tea cools when the person leaves. It's a perfect proverb for this scene.

t_ch: 装饰在柜子上的猫头鹰们盯着你，窃窃私语。
t_en: The owls set in the drawer stare at you, whispering among themselves.

t_ch: 这些柜子的密码锁看上去就像猫头鹰的眼睛。你试着拨动码盘，却没有半点反应。
t_en: The combination locks on these drawers look just like the eyes of the owls. You try turning the numbered dials, but receive no response.

t_ch: 这屋子的主人想必有许多珍宝要收纳——不然他为何要摆这么多柜子？
t_en: The master of this house must have kept many valuables — why would he have so many drawers out otherwise?

t_ch: 桌面被整理得干净又整齐，仿佛一尊供人观赏的标本。
t_en: The table is spick and span, like a specimen sample to be admired.

t_ch: 桌上的书籍没有半点灰尘，但你确信它们已经许久不曾被翻动。
t_en: There isn't a speck of dust on the books on the table, but you're sure they have not been touched in a long time.

t_ch: 精心划定的一小块空间，具有让人静下心来阅读的魔力。
t_en: A small space that has been delicately delineated — it has some alluring charm that calms you down when reading.

t_ch: 被大大小小、奇形怪状的钟表所环绕的角落，或许是「钟表匠」曾经的工作台。
t_en: A corner surrounded by clocks big and small, of all strange shapes and sizes. Perhaps it was once the Watchmaker's workbench.

t_ch: 无论这张桌曾经的主人是谁，他肯定不爱整理卫生。
t_en: No matter who the master of this table was, he was definitely not a kempt person.

t_ch: 如山的杂物堆积在办公桌表面，皮皮西人甚至很难从中露出自己的脑袋。
t_en: Piles of clutter are strewn across the office desk. A Pepeshi person would have found it hard to poke their head through the mess.

t_ch: 扭曲如漩涡的钟表，它所指的时间也混乱如麻。
t_en: A watch that swirls like a vortex — the time it tells is also a jumbled chaos.

t_ch: 混沌的时钟，怪诞诡谲一如其所在的混沌梦境。
t_en: A chaotic timepiece, strange and absurd, just like the disordered Dreamscape in which it exists.

t_ch: 这怪诞的梦究竟何时才能醒来？钟表和你一样想知道答案。
t_en: When will you rouse from this strange dream? Just like you, the clock wishes to know the answer.

t_ch: 你不会从里面找到任何有价值的物品，省省这份力气吧。
t_en: You will not find anything of value here. Save your energy.

t_ch: 柜子上的钟表永远指向了某个时刻，你不确定它代表着什么。
t_en: The clock on the drawer is permanently pointing at a certain moment, and you're not sure what it means.

t_ch: 梦境是现实印象的投射，而这个梦的主人对柜子的印象并不深刻。
t_en: The Dreamscape is a projection of reality's impression, and to this dream's master, the impression of the drawer isn't deep.

t_ch: 空气中满是令人平静的木头气息——不是活的，而是原生的、经过碾压和漂白的木制纸张。
t_en: The air is filled with oaken scent that soothes the nerves — not alive, but all-natural, compressed and bleached paper made from wood.

t_ch: 书籍与书架砌成了屋子的墙面，随着视线不断向上延伸。
t_en: The books and bookshelf have become the house's walls, rising to heights as far as the eyes see.

t_ch: 这陈腐木板与纸张所铸就的阶梯将通往哪里——愈发混沌的梦境、尚存余温的幻影、还是某段死去已久的回忆？
t_en: Where does this staircase made of withered wooden boards and paper go... To an even more chaotic dream, a phantom that lingers with warmth, or some long-dead memory?

t_ch: 即便以梦境的尺度来看，这里的藏书数量也堪称夸张。
t_en: Even by Dreamscape measurements, the books stashed here border on hyperbole.

t_ch: 书是人类进步的阶梯——在这里，它的确堆成了一座向上的阶梯。
t_en: Books are humanity's staircase to progress. Here, they have literally become a staircase that leads upwards.

t_ch: 书中字迹如同这梦境本身一般，混沌凌乱、无从分辨。
t_en: The writing in the books is akin to this Dreamscape itself: Chaotic, disorderly, and incomprehensible.

t_ch: 随手堆砌于此的书籍，也可能是被抛弃了——不知怎地，「抛弃」一词在这个梦中令人格外难受。
t_en: The books could have either been casually stacked here, or simply discarded — for some reason, the word "discarded" makes one feel particularly sad in this dream.

t_ch: 富有艺术气息的长桌。从这屋子的布置和藏书来看，主人的品味还算不错。
t_en: A long table brimming with artistic flair. From the looks of this house's layout and library, its master had great taste.

t_ch: 过去的某个时刻，这里应该发生了什么事情。可能是一场离别，亦或别的什么催人泪下的场合。
t_en: In some past moment, something must have happened here. Perhaps a separation, or some other tragically tearful scene.

t_ch: 你随手从书堆里抽出一本，封面的标题处歪歪扭扭写着：「存在与钟表维修技术」。
t_en: You casually pull out a book from a pile, and near the cover title, something is written haphazardly: "Existence and Watch Repair Techniques."

t_ch: 你试着拽了拽这把椅子，纹丝不动。看来它已经被永远定格在了这里。
t_en: You pull at this chair, but it doesn't budge. Looks like it's been permanently stuck here.

t_ch: 如果足够有闲心，你甚至可以用这把椅子锻炼肱二头肌。
t_en: If you were feeling idle, you could even use this chair to give your biceps a workout.

t_ch: 可惜周围没有梯子，不然你真的很想试试坐上去，悬浮在空中的感觉肯定很棒。
t_en: A pity there are no ladders around here, or you might really think of a way to sit in it — being suspended in midair must feel really exciting.

t_ch: 吊灯张开翅膀飞翔，书本在下方笨拙地追赶——光是看着这一幕，你就感到脖子一阵酸痛。
t_en: The chandelier spreads its wings and flies, the books clumsily pursuing below — the sheer sight of this is causing a sore pinch in your neck.

t_ch: 这盏灯飞得如此之高，以至于它的打开和关闭都成问题。
t_en: This lamp is flying so high that trying to turn it on and off is going to be a problem.

t_ch: 你很好奇，吊灯浮在那个位置究竟是为了照亮什么？
t_en: You're curious: What exactly is the chandelier illuminating in that position?

t_ch: 柔软的沙发，仿佛童年最甜美的梦。
t_en: A soft sofa akin to the sweetest dreams of childhood.

t_ch: 这座沙发的尺寸可供多人落座，但现在已经空无一人。
t_en: This sofa's size can seat multiple people, but it is completely empty now.

t_ch: 如果再多几个孩子，这间屋子或许会变得热闹又温馨。
t_en: If there were more children, this house would perhaps be more lively and cozy.

t_ch: 这应当是属于一个孩子的房间，看似温馨，却令人感到不安。
t_en: This should have belonged to a child. It looks warm, but fills one with a sense of unease.

t_ch: 积木们零散地堆放着，再无孩子的手将它们拿起。
t_en: The blocks are scattered all over, never again picked up by a child's hand.

t_ch: 毫无生气的游乐角，再无孩子的笑声将它填满。
t_en: A playtime corner devoid of life, never again filled with the gaiety of a child's laughter.

t_ch: 尚未拆封的盒子，里面藏着孩子所能想象到的最棒礼物。
t_en: An unopened box, inside it stashed with all the best gifts that a child could think of.

t_ch: 「不可以打开盒子！」在指尖即将触碰到礼物盒的那个瞬间，一声孱弱的悲鸣在你脑海中炸响，转瞬即逝。
t_en: "You can't open this box!" Just as your fingers brush against the present, a frail wail explodes in your mind, and then it is gone.

t_ch: 为庆祝某事而准备的礼物盒，本该属于一场热闹的聚会。
t_en: A present prepared for the celebration of a certain event. This should have belonged to a fun party.

t_ch: 无法开启的电视机。漆黑屏幕仿佛濒死者涣散的眼睛，空洞地映出四周明艳的色彩与光影。
t_en: A television set that can't be turned on. The pitch-black screen resembles the glazed eyes of a dying person, hollowly reflecting the splendid colors and light around it.

t_ch: 它再也无法放映孩子们喜欢的节目了。
t_en: It will never again show the programs that children loved.

t_ch: 电视上拴着两个无精打采的气球，可能是某次聚会的残余物。
t_en: Two listless balloons are secured to the television set — possible remnants of some party.

t_ch: 被气球吊死的钢琴。偶尔还会发出几声沙哑的、不和谐的颤音。
t_en: A piano lynched by balloons, occasionally emitting raspy, discordant trills.

t_ch: 你很好奇，悬在空中的钢琴是否能发出和平时相同的声响，但似乎没有机会尝试了。
t_en: You're curious: Can a piano suspended in midair make the sounds it usually makes? But it seems you won't have a chance to try it out.

t_ch: 一台价值不菲的钢琴，看来住在这间屋里的孩子受到了良好的音乐教育。
t_en: A very expensive piano. It seems that the child of this house received excellent music education.

t_ch: 没有阳光的梦里，只有假花才能茁壮生长。
t_en: In the sunless dream, only fake flowers are allowed to grow.

t_ch: 这抹为惨淡光线所浸透的翠绿，并未能带来任何生机，反倒平添几许寒意。
t_en: This emerald green soaked in bleak light does not bring about life, but rather adds a shroud of chill instead.

t_ch: 架子上摆着许多书本，内容大部分是天马行空的童话。
t_en: Many books are on display on the shelf, in which most of them are written fantastical fairy tales.

t_ch: 架上的书籍没有半点灰尘，但你确信它们已经许久不曾被翻动。
t_en: There isn't a speck of dust on the books on the shelf, but you're sure they have not been touched in a long time.

t_ch: 有棱有角的雕像，按照孩童的喜好进行了设计，似乎是折纸小鸟的原型。
t_en: A statue with edges and corners, folded according to a child's design. Looks like the shape of an origami bird.

t_ch: 威武的鸟类雕像，或许是梦境的某种象征。
t_en: A majestic statue of a bird. Perhaps it's a symbol of the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 坚固的箱子保护着稚子最为重要的秘宝。
t_en: A sturdy chest that protects a child's most important treasure.
